notes.anchor: on the day before the Kalends of January
notes.note: Pridie Kalendas Ianuarias is the day immediately before the first of the month, i.e. 31 December 50 BC; the Kalends marked the start of each Roman month.
Pompey owned an estate in the Alban district (the Albanum) in the hills southeast of Rome; Cicero plans to call there on his way up to the city as the crisis deepens.

notes.anchor: the loyalists, as people suppose them
notes.note: The boni ("loyalists" or "the good men") were the conservative senatorial party Cicero identified with; he notes bitterly that they are no longer united even among themselves.
A quartana was a quartan fever, a malarial ague recurring every fourth day; Cicero closes by urging Atticus to throw it off, the Greek imperative lending a light, almost medical touch.
